variants.name: Variant 1
variants.text:
Bitwise Matching

In the following questions assume the variable x is a signed integer and that the machine uses two's complement representation.

The >> operator behaves as an arithmetic shift.

Match each description on the left with a line of code on the right.

  1. x > 0 {1:MULTICHOICE:=a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~%0%f}
  2. x != 0 {2:MULTICHOICE:%0%a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~=e~%0%f}
  1. a) ((x >> W) & 0x1) | !x
  2. b) !(!x | x >> W)
  3. c) 1 << W
  4. d) (x << W) >> W
  5. e) ((~x+1 | x) >> W) & 1
  6. f) ~x - (TMin + TMax)

variants.name: Variant 2
variants.text:
Bitwise Matching

In the following questions assume the variable x is a signed integer and that the machine uses two's complement representation.

The >> operator behaves as an arithmetic shift.

Match each description on the left with a line of code on the right.

  1. x > 0 {1:MULTICHOICE:=a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~%0%f}
  2. x != 0 {2:MULTICHOICE:%0%a~%0%b~%0%c~=d~%0%e~%0%f}
  1. a) ((x >> W) & 0x1) | !x
  2. b) (x << W) >> W
  3. c) 1 << W
  4. d) ((~x+1 | x) >> W) & 1
  5. e) !(!x | x >> W)
  6. f) ~x - (TMin + TMax)

variants.name: Variant 3
variants.text:
Bitwise Matching

In the following questions assume the variable x is a signed integer and that the machine uses two's complement representation.

The >> operator behaves as an arithmetic shift.

Match each description on the left with a line of code on the right.

  1. x > 0 {1:MULTICHOICE:%0%a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~=f}
  2. x != 0 {2:MULTICHOICE:=a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~%0%f}
  1. a) ((~x+1 | x) >> W) & 1
  2. b) ~x - (TMin + TMax)
  3. c) (x << W) >> W
  4. d) !(!x | x >> W)
  5. e) 1 << W
  6. f) ((x >> W) & 0x1) | !x

variants.name: Variant 4
variants.text:
Bitwise Matching

In the following questions assume the variable x is a signed integer and that the machine uses two's complement representation.

The >> operator behaves as an arithmetic shift.

Match each description on the left with a line of code on the right.

  1. x > 0 {1:MULTICHOICE:%0%a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~=f}
  2. x != 0 {2:MULTICHOICE:=a~%0%b~%0%c~%0%d~%0%e~%0%f}
  1. a) ((~x+1 | x) >> W) & 1
  2. b) (x << W) >> W
  3. c) ~x - (TMin + TMax)
  4. d) 1 << W
  5. e) !(!x | x >> W)
  6. f) ((x >> W) & 0x1) | !x
